name: Pad Kra Pao
description: A Thai basil stir-fry made with ground pork, chicken, or beef, garlic, Thai bird’s eye chilies, savory sauces, holy basil, jasmine rice, crispy fried egg, and spicy prik nam pla on the side.
recipeNotes: Holy basil gives pad kra pao its classic peppery flavor. Thai basil works in a pinch, but the dish will taste sweeter and more anise-like.
For a spicier version, use 6 to 10 chilies in the stir-fry and extra sliced chilies in the prik nam pla.
recipeIngredient: 450 grams ground pork, chicken, or beef
3 to 6 Thai bird’s eye chilies, or to taste
1 teaspoon dark soy sauce, optional, for color
1 to 2 tablespoons water or chicken stock
3 tablespoons fish sauce, for prik nam pla
1 to 2 tablespoons lime juice, for prik nam pla
2 to 4 Thai bird’s eye chilies, thinly sliced, for prik nam pla
1 small garlic clove, thinly sliced or minced, for prik nam pla
1/2 teaspoon sugar, optional, for prik nam pla
3 to 4 tablespoons oil, for crispy fried egg

itemListElement.text: In a small bowl, mix fish sauce, lime juice, sliced chilies, garlic, and sugar if using.
Let the prik nam pla sit while you cook so the flavors infuse.
Using a mortar and pestle, pound the garlic and Thai bird’s eye chilies into a rough paste. Alternatively, mince them finely with a knife.
Heat 2 tablespoons neutral oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic-chili paste and stir-fry for 15 to 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the ground meat and break it up with a spatula.
Stir-fry until the meat is mostly cooked through and lightly browned.
Add oyster sauce, soy sauce, fish sauce, dark soy sauce if using, sugar, and a splash of water or chicken stock.
Stir-fry for 1 to 2 minutes until glossy and well coated.
Turn off the heat, add the holy basil leaves, and toss until just wilted from the residual heat.
Heat 3 to 4 tablespoons oil in a small pan until hot.
Crack in an egg and fry until the edges are bubbly and crisp while the yolk remains runny.
Spoon hot oil over the whites to help them puff.
Repeat with 1 egg per serving, if desired.
Spoon the pad kra pao over cooked jasmine rice.
Top each serving with a crispy fried egg, if using.
Serve with prik nam pla on the side.
